Stopwatch Gang.description: The Stopwatch Gang was a group of three Canadian bank robbers active from 1974 to 1980. They robbed over 140 banks in the United States and Canada, stealing about $15 million Canadian. Known for meticulous planning, non-violent methods, and completing heists in under two minutes. The gang was named by the FBI after a member wore a stopwatch during robberies.

Mullen Gang.description: The Mullen Gang was an Irish-American street gang based in South Boston, Massachusetts, active from the 1950s until 1972. It engaged in racketeering, armed robbery, auto theft, burglary, extortion, loansharking, illegal gambling, and murder. The gang derived its name from a commemorative plaque to war hero John Joseph Mullen. After a decade-long gang war with the Killeen Gang, both groups were consolidated under the Winter Hill Gang in 1972.

descriptions: The Killeen Gang and the Mullen Gang were at war in the early 1970s.
edges.evidence_quote: In the early 1970s, another mob war was taking place in South Boston between two other Irish-American gangs: the Killeen Gang ... and the Mullen Gang ...

descriptions: The Mullen Gang fought a gang war against the Killeen Gang for control of criminal rackets in South Boston.
edges.evidence_quote: After a decade-long gang war against another Irish mob group, the Killeen Gang, for control of criminal rackets in South Boston, both gangs were consolidated under the control of the Winter Hill Gang in 1972.

descriptions: The Patriarca crime family mediated the dispute between the Mullen Gang and the Killeen Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Patrick Nee arranged for their dispute to be mediated by Howie Winter, the godfather of the Irish-American Winter Hill Gang of Somerville and Joseph Russo of the Patriarca crime family.

descriptions: The Mullen Gang was allied with the Winter Hill Gang, as stated in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Winter Hill Gang

descriptions: Winter Hill Gang absorbed the remnants of the Mullen Gang after the gang wars.
edges.evidence_quote: absorbing the remnants of the Mullen and Killeen gangs of South Boston following a series of Irish gang wars

descriptions: Winter Hill Gang absorbed the Mullen Gang after conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: absorbing the remnants of the Mullen and Killeen gangs of South Boston

descriptions: Winter Hill Gang absorbed the remnants of the Mullen Gang after conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: absorbing the remnants of the Mullen and Killeen gangs of South Boston following a series of Irish gang wars

Baltimore Crew.description: The Baltimore Crew was an Italian-American organized crime group that operated in Baltimore, Maryland, from about 1900 until the 1990s. Originally independent, it became a faction of the Gambino crime family in 1955. The crew was involved in gambling, extortion, prostitution, loan sharking, fraud, drug trafficking, murder, and corruption. It was led by the Corbi family for much of its existence.

descriptions: A member of the Baltimore Crew murdered a reputed Camorra member.
edges.evidence_quote: Patsy Corbi was sentenced to life imprisonment for the murder of local barber Frank Naples, a reputed Camorra member.

descriptions: The Corbi family functionally headed the Baltimore Crew after 1920.
edges.evidence_quote: The Corbi family acquiesced to the Gambino relationship, but maintained local leaders, simply answering to and accessing support from Morici and his New York Gambino connections.

descriptions: The D'Urso family originally led the Baltimore Crew.
edges.evidence_quote: It was originally an independent organization, led by the D'Urso family until the Corbi takeover in the 1920s.

descriptions: The Baltimore Crew became a faction of the Gambino crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: The Baltimore Crew was an Italian-American organized-crime group that ultimately became a faction of the Gambino crime family

descriptions: The PKK and MKP are both members of the Peoples' United Revolutionary Movement.
edges.evidence_quote: In March 2016, the PKK helped to launch the Peoples' United Revolutionary Movement with nine other Kurdish and Turkish revolutionary leftist, socialist and communist groups (including the TKP/ML , THKP-C/MLSPB , MKP

Trinitarios.description: Dominican American criminal organization founded in 1993 on Rikers Island, New York City, by Leonides Sierra and Julio Marine. Initially formed to protect Dominicans and other Hispanics in prison, it expanded into drug trafficking, murder, and extortion across the northeastern US and internationally. Despite major prosecutions in 2009-2014, the group has experienced a resurgence with internal factional conflicts.

descriptions: Bloods are allied with Trinitarios.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: Trinitarios (organización criminal)

descriptions: Trinitarios are DDP's biggest rival since early 2000s, but were once united in the 1990s.
edges.evidence_quote: The Trinitarios are DDP's biggest rival gang since the early 2000s, they were once united in the 1990s.

descriptions: DDP and Trinitarios were once united in the 1990s.
edges.evidence_quote: they were once united in the 1990s

descriptions: Infobox lists Trinitarios as rival.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Asian Boyz, Bloods, Crips, Latin Kings, Sureños, Trinitarios

descriptions: Crips are listed as rivals of Trinitarios in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Crips, Dominicans Don't Play, Tiny Rascal Gang

descriptions: Dominicans Don't Play are listed as rivals of Trinitarios in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Crips, Dominicans Don't Play, Tiny Rascal Gang

descriptions: Trinitarios and Gangster Disciples are engaged in a feud with multiple shootings.
edges.evidence_quote: A Haverhill detective wrote that the arrests were in connection with an ongoing feud (a gang war) between the Trinitarios and their rivals, the Gangster Disciples, that had resulted in multiple shootings.

descriptions: A Trinitarios member was punished for associating with MS-13, indicating hostility between the groups.
edges.evidence_quote: Seven Barcelona-based members were convicted of using a kitchen knife to carve a fifty centimeter 'X' into the back of a gang member deemed a traitor for associating with MS-13 on March 21, 2012.

descriptions: Tiny Rascal Gang are listed as rivals of Trinitarios in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Crips, Dominicans Don't Play, Tiny Rascal Gang

descriptions: Trinitarios and Ñetas engaged in a brawl in Madrid in 2014.
edges.evidence_quote: On the same day as the ruling – May 11, 2014 – a brawl occurred in Madrid involving Trinitarios and Ñetas, leading to the arrests of twenty-six gang members.

descriptions: Ñetas and Trinitarios engaged in a brawl in Madrid, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: On May 11, 2014, a brawl occurred in Madrid involving Ñetas and Trinitarios which resulted in two injuries and the arrests of twenty-six gang members.

descriptions: Ñetas and Trinitarios fought in Madrid in 2014.
edges.evidence_quote: El 11 de mayo de 2014, se produjo una pelea en Madrid entre Ñetas y Trinitarios que resultó en dos heridos y la detención de veintiséis pandilleros.

edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: Cartel Gente Nueva De Michoacán

descriptions: Hells Angels founded the Renegades as a puppet gang.
edges.evidence_quote: The Hells Angels founded a puppet gang in Prince George known as the Renegades who in turn founded another puppet gang known as the Crew.

descriptions: Renegades Motorcycle Club listed as rival in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: ... Renegades Motorcycle Club(Canada) ...

descriptions: Independent Soldiers and Renegades cooperated in drug trade as Hells Angels flunkies.
edges.evidence_quote: The Independent Soldier chapter in Prince George came to dominate the drug trade in that city alongside the Renegades who were also 'Hells Angels flunkies'.

descriptions: Members of the Renegades MC shot and killed an Outlaws member.
edges.evidence_quote: Frank Rego Vital of Roberta, an Outlaws member, was shot and killed in an early morning gunfight on June 24, 2007, in the parking lot of The Crazy Horse Saloon strip club in Forest Park by two members of the Renegades MC

descriptions: Renegades founded the Crew as a puppet gang.
edges.evidence_quote: The Hells Angels founded a puppet gang in Prince George known as the Renegades who in turn founded another puppet gang known as the Crew.

Gang des Tractions Avant.description: A criminal gang active in the Pigalle quarter of Paris, composed of former Carlingue militia members, lapsed police officers, and criminals from the French Resistance. Named after their preferred vehicle, the Citroën 11CV 'Traction', the gang operated in the mid-20th century and included notorious figures such as Pierre Loutrel and Émile Buisson. Their methods influenced later gangs and popular culture.

descriptions: The Gang des Tractions Avant derived its methods from the Bonnot Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Its methods were largely derived from those of the Bonnot Gang

descriptions: The Gang des Tractions Avant was composed of former members of the Carlingue militia.
edges.evidence_quote: made up of surviving members of the Carlingue militia

descriptions: The methods of the Gang des Tractions Avant were continued by the Gang des Postiches.
edges.evidence_quote: were mostly continued by a number of other gangs, notably the gang des postiches

descriptions: A faction of Caltagirone Mafia supported the Mazzei clan in a coup attempt against Santapaola.
edges.evidence_quote: His nephew, Gesualdo La Rocca, supported an attempted coup by the rival Mazzei clan to displace the Santapaola leadership in Catania.

descriptions: The Catania faction of Cursoti is closely linked to the Mazzei clan.
edges.evidence_quote: la seconda, detta dei Cursoti catanesi , era guidata da Giuseppe Garozzo detto Pippo 'u maritatu, saldamente legato al clan storicamente capeggiato da Santo Mazzei detto ' u Carcagnusu .

descriptions: The 107th Street gang was under the protection of the Reina family, predecessor of the Lucchese family.
edges.evidence_quote: The 107th Street gang operated under the protection of Bronx–East Harlem family boss Gaetano "Tom" Reina .

descriptions: Phantom Riders revived Satan's Choice to enrage Black Diamond Riders, indicating hostility.
edges.evidence_quote: Phantom Riders president Bernie Guindon subsequently revived the name and patch of the disbanded club as a way to enrage the Black Diamond Riders.

descriptions: Phantom Riders revived Satan's Choice name; later merged into Satan's Choice.
edges.evidence_quote: Phantom Riders president Bernie Guindon subsequently revived the name and patch of the disbanded club as a way to enrage the Black Diamond Riders.

descriptions: The Beach Gang and Downtown Gang were rivals.
edges.evidence_quote: Their gang was a rival of Johnny Jack Nounes and George Musey's Downtown Gang

descriptions: Quinn mentored and partnered with the Maceo brothers, who later succeeded him.
edges.evidence_quote: Quinn became a mentor, and then a partner, to Sam and Rosario Maceo , who later took over the Galveston underworld.

descriptions: The Downtown Gang and Beach Gang were rivals, fighting for control of Galveston.
edges.evidence_quote: They were the rival gang of Ollie Quinn and Dutch Voight's Beach Gang.

descriptions: The two gangs occasionally cooperated on rum-running operations.
edges.evidence_quote: Every now and then for business purposes the gangs would negotiate like the majority of the mob does with their counterparts. Dutch Voight would bring some guys and go on rum-runs with George Musey.

descriptions: The Maceo brothers were initially associated with the Beach Gang before taking control.
edges.evidence_quote: The Maceo brothers got their start by being associated with the Beach Gang

descriptions: Srigala Hitam was a small unit that took allegiance through SP88.
edges.evidence_quote: This saw small units of the lasykar who had taken some allegiance to the Bambu Runcing Division through the SP88 to take up the characteristics of pre-war gangs. These small units took up names such as Pasukan Siluman (Ghost Squad), Srigala Hitam (Black Jackal), Pemotong Leher (Neck Cutters) and Garuda Putih (White Eagle).

descriptions: Klutas, leader of the College Kidnappers, had connections with John Dillinger's gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Klutas had connections with John Dillinger and with the Chicago crime world.

descriptions: Giffoni is a splinter group from Pecoraro-Renna.
edges.evidence_quote: All'interno del clan si sarebbero verificate delle scissioni, a séguito delle quali sarebbero sorti due gruppi autonomi facenti capo alle famiglie Trimarco, Frappaolo e Giffoni.

Aizukotetsu-kai.description: A yakuza organization based in Kyoto, Japan, founded in 1868 by Senkichi Kousaka. Its name derives from the Aizu region and 'Kotetsu', a type of Japanese sword. In 1992 it was among the first syndicates designated under Japan's anti-boryokudan laws, and it challenged the legislation in court. At its peak in 1993 it had 1,600 members, but membership has since declined to about 40.

descriptions: The Aizukotetsu-kai allied with the Yamaguchi-gumi in October 2005.
edges.evidence_quote: In October 2005, the group allied with the Sixth Yamaguchi-gumi , Japan's largest yakuza clan now led by Kenichi Shinoda (Oyabun) and his second-in-command (Wakagashira) Kiyoshi Takayama .

descriptions: Aizukotetsu-kai is listed as an ally of Yamaguchi-gumi.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Inagawa-kai, Soai-kai, Matsuba-kai, Kyōsei-kai, Aizukotetsu-kai, Toa-kai, Fukuhaku-kai, Gōda-ikka, Shinwa-kai

descriptions: The Gianola brothers feuded with the Adamo gang over rackets.
edges.evidence_quote: Between 1912 and 1913, the Gianola brothers took over the Wyandotte area rackets gang starting a feud with Wyandotte Mafia boss Vito Adamo.

descriptions: The Gianola brothers and Vitale gang fought a war for control of Detroit's Italian underworld.
edges.evidence_quote: In late 1918, Vitale broke away from the Gianola gang and established himself independently, aligning himself with the remnants of the Bosco group. What became known as the 'Gianola-Vitale War' emerged as one of the bloodiest battles in the history of the Detroit Mafia.

descriptions: Hells Angels absorbed the Hackers gang by patching them over to form a chapter.
edges.evidence_quote: The Hells Angels founded their first chapters in New York state on December 5, 1969 by "patching over" the Aliens motorcycle gang of New York City and the Hackers biker gang in Rochester

Venice 13.description: Venice 13 is a Mexican American street gang based in the Oakwood neighborhood of Venice, Los Angeles, California. Founded in the 1920s or 1930s, it is one of the earliest gangs in the city and is affiliated with the Sureños and the Mexican Mafia. The gang is known for its long-running rivalry with the Culver City Boys 13 and has been involved in drug trafficking and violent conflicts. It remains active as of the present.

descriptions: Infobox lists Venice 13 as a rival.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Aryan Brotherhood , Bloods , Florencia 13 , Latin Kings , People Nation , Playboys , Santa Monica 13 , Tiny Rascal Gang , United Blood Nation , Venice 13

descriptions: Culver City Boys are rivals with Venice 13.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Santa Monica 13 , 18th Street Gang , Venice Shoreline Crips Venice 13

descriptions: Santa Monica 13 and Venice 13 are allies after a truce.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Mexican Mafia , Sureños , Venice 13 (after the truce)

descriptions: Venice 13 has rivalry with 18th Street gang.
edges.evidence_quote: As well as 18th Street gang (particularly the 106th St in Inglewood), as well as the Grave Yard Gangsta Crips in Santa Monica and Culver City 13 in the Mar Vista neighborhood.

descriptions: 18th Street gang is listed as an ally of Venice 13 in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Lennox13 , PlayBoys Gangster Crips , Santa Monica 13 , 18th Street gang , Venice Shoreline Crips

descriptions: Venice 13 has deadly street violence with Culver City Boys 13.
edges.evidence_quote: It is widely known for its deadly street violence with fellow Mexican-American gang, the Culver City Boys 13.

descriptions: Culver City Boys 13 is listed as a rival of Venice 13 in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Culver City Boys 13

descriptions: Venice 13 and Culver City Boys 13 temporarily united against Venice Shoreline Crips.
edges.evidence_quote: Venice 13 and the Culver City gang went as far as to set aside their own fight to unite against the Shorelines.

descriptions: Venice 13 has rivalry with Grave Yard Gangsta Crips.
edges.evidence_quote: As well as 18th Street gang (particularly the 106th St in Inglewood), as well as the Grave Yard Gangsta Crips in Santa Monica and Culver City 13 in the Mar Vista neighborhood.

descriptions: Lennox13 is listed as an ally of Venice 13 in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Lennox13 , PlayBoys Gangster Crips , Santa Monica 13 , 18th Street gang , Venice Shoreline Crips

edges.evidence_quote: It is affiliated with the Sureños and the Mexican Mafia.

descriptions: PlayBoys Gangster Crips is listed as an ally of Venice 13 in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Lennox13 , PlayBoys Gangster Crips , Santa Monica 13 , 18th Street gang , Venice Shoreline Crips

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Lennox13 , PlayBoys Gangster Crips , Santa Monica 13 , 18th Street gang , Venice Shoreline Crips

descriptions: Venice 13 was rival with Santa Monica 13, then settled truce and formed alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: Venice 13's main rivals were the Santa Monica 13 and Santa Monica 17th St gangs, however they both settled a truce and formed an alliance.

descriptions: Venice 13 was rival with Santa Monica 17th St, then settled truce and formed alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: Venice 13's main rivals were the Santa Monica 13 and Santa Monica 17th St gangs, however they both settled a truce and formed an alliance.

descriptions: Venice 13 is affiliated with Sureños.
edges.evidence_quote: It is affiliated with the Sureños and the Mexican Mafia.

descriptions: Venice 13 fought gang wars against Venice Shoreline Crips.
edges.evidence_quote: It was amongst the first Mexican gangs to fight gang wars against African-American gangs, especially the local Venice Shoreline Crips which were founded in the mid-1970s.

descriptions: Venice Shoreline Crips is listed as an ally of Venice 13 in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Lennox13 , PlayBoys Gangster Crips , Santa Monica 13 , 18th Street gang , Venice Shoreline Crips

descriptions: Venice 13 and Venice Shoreline Crips agreed to a cease fire in 1994.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1994 Venice 13 and the Shorelines bargained for a cease fire, after a war that left 55 people dead and many more wounded.

descriptions: The Shoreline Crips and Venice 13 fought a war over the drug trade, then agreed to a cease-fire.
edges.evidence_quote: a war broke out between the Shorelines and Venice 13 over control of the Venice drug trade until a cease-fire was arranged between the two gangs

descriptions: Lobos members joined Hells Angels in a patch-over ceremony.
edges.evidence_quote: On 29 December 2000, most of the Ontario biker gangs in a mass 'patch over' ... joined the Hells Angels.

descriptions: Lobos members patched over to the Hells Angels, indicating absorption.
edges.evidence_quote: This resulted in 168 members of the Para-Dice Riders, Satan's Choice, Lobos and Last Chance "patching" to the Angels.

descriptions: Satan's Choice allied with Lobos in 1981.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1981, Satan's Choice made an alliance with the Lobos and the Chosen Few gangs to improve their bargaining power against their rivals, the Outlaws.

descriptions: Satan's Choice, Vagabonds, and Lobos cooperated to manufacture and distribute methamphetamine.
edges.evidence_quote: A report by the police Organized Crime Committee from 1989 stated that Satan's Choice, the Vagabonds and the Lobos were working closely to manufacture and distribute methamphetamine.

descriptions: Satan's Choice and Lobos both patched over to Hells Angels on the same day, but no direct relationship between them is stated.
edges.evidence_quote: Satan's Choice, along with most of the other major Ontario biker gangs, including the Loners, Lobos, and Para-Dice Riders, 'patched over' to the Hells Angels on December 29, 2000.

descriptions: Tempel ov Blood infiltrated and took over Atomwaffen Division, a structural takeover.
edges.evidence_quote: The ToB infiltrated and effectively took over the AWD, beginning in 2017.

descriptions: ITS leader claimed influence from Tempel ov Blood.
edges.evidence_quote: A leader of the ITS claimed to have been influenced by the satanic fascist group the Tempel ov Blood .

Lucchese crime family Brooklyn faction.description: The Lucchese crime family's Brooklyn faction is a group of Italian-American mobsters within the Lucchese crime family, based in Brooklyn and Staten Island. It was formed in the early 2000s by merging the Vario Crew and the 19th Hole Crew. The faction is involved in racketeering, murder, and other illegal activities. It remains active as part of the larger Lucchese family.

descriptions: The Brooklyn faction is a sub-unit of the Lucchese crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: The Lucchese crime family's Brooklyn faction is a group of Italian-American mobsters within the Lucchese crime family

descriptions: The Vario Crew is a crew within the Lucchese crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: The Vario Crew ... had predominantly been based in Brooklyn neighborhoods of Canarsie and Flatlands. The crew was controlled by Caporegime Paul Vario during the early 1960s into the early 1980s

descriptions: The Bensonhurst Crew is a crew within the Lucchese crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: The 19th Hole Crew , sometimes called Bensonhurst Crew , was based in Bensonhurst, Brooklyn , before it was merged into the Brooklyn faction.

descriptions: The Brooklyn faction is a part of the Lucchese crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: The Lucchese crime family's Brooklyn faction is a group of Italian-American mobsters within the Lucchese crime family

descriptions: Burke's crew cooperated with Vario's crew in hijacking operations.
edges.evidence_quote: One of Vario's associates was James "Jimmy" Burke , an Irish gangster who ran his own crew of hijackers that would pay off truck drivers and then unload the goods at a warehouse Vario controlled.

edges.source: Gang de la Brise de Mer
descriptions: Brise de Mer is suspected of killing members of Armata Corsa.
edges.evidence_quote: The "Brise de Mer" is also suspected of involvement in the 2001 murders of members of Armata Corsa , a separatist Corsican armed group.

descriptions: Mkhedrioni opposed the Bagramyan Battalion.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: Gamsakhurdia's government in exile , Abkhazia , Bagramyan Battalion , Confederation of Mountain Peoples of the Caucasus

Venice Shoreline Crips.description: The Venice Shoreline Crips is a set of the Crips gang alliance based in Venice, Los Angeles, California. Formed in the late 1960s in the Oakwood neighborhood, it became heavily involved in narcotics trafficking during the 1980s crack epidemic. The gang has been engaged in violent conflicts with rival gangs such as Culver City 13 and Venice 13. Despite law enforcement efforts, the gang remains active with hundreds of members.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Santa Monica 13 , 18th Street Gang , Venice Shoreline Crips Venice 13

descriptions: Venice 13 and Venice Shoreline Crips agreed to a cease fire in 1994.

descriptions: The Shoreline Crips were in a war with Culver City 13 over territory.
edges.evidence_quote: the Shorelines warred with rival Culver City 13 after Shoreline cliques were pushed out of the Mar Vista Gardens projects

descriptions: UWSA is the armed wing of the United Wa State Party.
edges.evidence_quote: ethnic Wa cadres under the command of Bao Youxiang announced their split from the party and the founding of the United Wa State Party and United Wa State Army

descriptions: UWSA is the military wing of UWSP.
edges.evidence_quote: The United Wa State Army ... is the military wing of the United Wa State Party (UWSP)

descriptions: The Petit Bar Gang and Mattei clan are two principal blocs in Corsican organized crime, with the Mattei clan seeking to unify groups.
edges.evidence_quote: The report highlighted their infiltration into economic sectors such as construction, hospitality and real estate, and noted the emergence of two principal blocs: one linked to the Petit Bar Gang in Ajaccio and another associated with the Mattei clan, which has sought to unify various groups.

descriptions: The infobox lists the Mattei clan as a rival of the Petit Bar Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Mattei clan , Movement for Self-Determination (Orsoni clan)

edges.evidence_quote: Herrera Organization

Secondigliano Alliance.description: The Secondigliano Alliance is a strategic alliance of Camorra clans based in Naples, Italy, founded in the late 1980s by Gennaro Licciardi, Edoardo Contini, and Francesco Mallardo. It controls drug trafficking and extortion rackets in many areas of Naples and is considered one of the two most powerful Camorra organizations. The alliance has branches in the Netherlands and Spain, involved in international drug trafficking and money laundering. Despite police crackdowns, it remains active.

descriptions: Giuliano and Alleanza di Secondigliano fought a violent clash in 1990.
edges.evidence_quote: faida tra i Giuliano e l'Alleanza di Secondigliano : violento scontro avvenuto tra i due potenti gruppi nel 1990

descriptions: The Aprea-Cuccaro clan made an agreement with the Secondigliano Alliance since the 1990s.
edges.evidence_quote: According to investigators, since the 1990s, the Aprea-Cuccaro clan has entered into an agreement with the Secondigliano Alliance.

descriptions: The Secondigliano Alliance is a powerful group within the Camorra.
edges.evidence_quote: the most powerful groups of the Camorra in the present day are the Mazzarella clan and the Secondigliano Alliance

descriptions: Secondigliano Alliance is a Camorra clan, part of the Camorra organization.
edges.evidence_quote: According to numerous 2019 reports, the most powerful clans of the Camorra are the Secondigliano Alliance and the Mazzarella clan .

descriptions: Casamonica has a high-level relationship with the Alleanza di Secondigliano through Salvatore Esposito.
edges.evidence_quote: Salvatore Esposito racconta un altro rapporto di livello della casata. Esposito, infatti, è figlio di Luigi, detto "Nacchella", braccio destro del defunto Gennaro Licciardi, detto "'a scimmia", uno dei re del narcotraffico, esponente di vertice dell'Alleanza di Secondigliano.

descriptions: The Di Biasi had friendships with bosses of the Alleanza di Secondigliano, implying cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: mal protetto dalle sue amicizie con i boss dell' Alleanza di Secondigliano

descriptions: Alleanza di Secondigliano supported Clan Marfella.
edges.evidence_quote: spalleggiato anche dall' Alleanza di Secondigliano

descriptions: Clan Mariano was at war with Alleanza di Secondigliano in the early 1990s.
edges.evidence_quote: Il clan dei Mariano fu in guerra prima con i Faiano (i Di Biasi) e poi con l' Alleanza di Secondigliano agli inizi degli anni novanta per il controllo dei Quartieri Spagnoli.

descriptions: The Misso clan fought against scissionists close to the Secondigliano clans.
edges.evidence_quote: un conflitto tra il clan Misso del Rione Sanità e alcuni scissionisti guidati da Salvatore Torino, vicino ai clan di Secondigliano

descriptions: Misso and Alleanza di Secondigliano fought a faida after the 1992 double murder.
edges.evidence_quote: faida tra i Misso e l'Alleanza di Secondigliano : faida portata avanti dal boss Giuseppe Misso e dai vertici dell' Alleanza di Secondigliano

descriptions: Misso, together with Mazzarella and Sarno, planned revenge against the Alleanza di Secondigliano.
edges.evidence_quote: che avevano chiare ragioni di odio nei confronti dell'Alleanza di Secondigliano, per un progetto di vendetta per l'omicidio della moglie, Assunta Sarno.

descriptions: Gaetano Bocchetti, co-founder of the clan, was the founder of the Alleanza di Secondigliano, indicating a structural tie.
edges.evidence_quote: Il clan prende il nome da Gennaro Sacco e Gaetano Bocchetti , quest'ultimo fautore dell' Alleanza di Secondigliano .

descriptions: Clan Sarno was at war with the Alleanza di Secondigliano.
edges.evidence_quote: entrò prima in guerra con i clan dell' Alleanza di Secondigliano

descriptions: Commisso 'ndrina and Secondigliano Alliance are allied in drug trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: The Commissos are known to have links with the Secondigliano Alliance ... Both organizations are allied in the trafficking and sale of cocaine and marijuana from South America, via the Netherlands.

descriptions: Commisso has close ties with Alleanza di Secondigliano for drug trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: Secondo gli inquirenti, la 'ndrina ha stretti rapporti con l' Alleanza di Secondigliano , un potente cartello della camorra .

descriptions: Contini clan is a founding member of the Secondigliano Alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: The Contini clan is also one of the founding clans of the Secondigliano Alliance

descriptions: Clan Contini is part of the Alleanza di Secondigliano.
edges.evidence_quote: Il clan Contini fa parte dell'«Alleanza di Secondigliano», insieme a clan Mallardo e clan Licciardi.

descriptions: De Luca Bossa clan allied with Secondigliano Alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: In the years when Teresa De Luca Bossa was in command, the clan also made an alliance with the powerful Secondigliano Alliance.

descriptions: Teresa De Luca Bossa was a member of the Alleanza di Secondigliano.
edges.evidence_quote: membro di quel cartello conosciuto come Alleanza di Secondigliano

descriptions: Di Lauro clan strengthened connections within the Secondigliano Alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: Their connections with other clans were strengthened in the Secondigliano Alliance.

descriptions: The Di Lauro family acts as external guarantors for the Alleanza di Secondigliano.
edges.evidence_quote: con gli stessi Di Lauro come garanti esterni

descriptions: The Di Lauro clan strengthened its alliances with the other clans of the Alleanza di Secondigliano.
edges.evidence_quote: la cosca rafforzò le sue alleanze con gli altri clan dell' Alleanza di Secondigliano

descriptions: Licciardi clan is a founding member of the Secondigliano Alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: He also became the founding member of the Secondigliano Alliance, a coalition of powerful Camorra clans which controls drug trafficking and extortion rackets in many suburbs of Naples.

descriptions: Clan Licciardi is part of the Alleanza di Secondigliano.
edges.evidence_quote: Il clan Contini fa parte dell'«Alleanza di Secondigliano», insieme a clan Mallardo e clan Licciardi.

descriptions: Clan Licciardi is a member of the Alleanza di Secondigliano cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: Il clan fa parte insieme alle famiglie Contini, Mallardo, Lo Russo, Ferone, Stabile, Prestieri, Bosti e Bocchetti del cartello camorristico denominato Alleanza di Secondigliano

descriptions: Lo Russo clan joined the Secondigliano Alliance after the NF dissolved.
edges.evidence_quote: After the end of the NF alliance, the Lo Russo clan joined the newly formed Secondigliano Alliance

descriptions: Mallardo clan is a member of the Secondigliano Alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: The Mallardo clan is also one of the clans that belongs to the Secondigliano Alliance

descriptions: Clan Mallardo is part of the Alleanza di Secondigliano.

descriptions: Mazzarella clan opposed the Secondigliano Alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: to oppose to the rising power of the Secondigliano Alliance

descriptions: Alleanza di Secondigliano ordered the killing of Francesco Mazzarella.
edges.evidence_quote: i mandanti furono i vertici dell' Alleanza di Secondigliano

descriptions: The Rinaldi clan has the Secondigliano Alliance as allies.
edges.evidence_quote: having as allies the Secondigliano Alliance

descriptions: Rinaldi clan is allied with Secondigliano Alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: The clan is allied with the powerful Secondigliano Alliance , decisive in making the Rinaldi one of the most powerful clans in the Camorra.

descriptions: The Sacco-Bocchetti-Lo Russo group left the Alleanza di Secondigliano.
edges.evidence_quote: il gruppo Sacco-Bocchetti-Lo Russo, uscito dall’ Alleanza di Secondigliano

descriptions: The Amato-Pagano clan controlled drug channels, while the Alleanza di Secondigliano was involved in other activities; no direct relationship stated.
edges.evidence_quote: mentre i canali della droga restavano sotto il controllo di altri clan, soprattutto gli Amato-Pagano

descriptions: The Aprea-Cuccaro clan is a founding clan and ally of the Secondigliano Alliance.

descriptions: After splitting, the Lo Russo clan fought a gang war against the Secondigliano Alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: The Lo Russo clan eventually split from the alliance, leading to a violent gang war against the other clans which claimed 120 lives in Naples and the surrounding region.

descriptions: Lo Russo clan was initially an ally of the Secondigliano Alliance, then split and became hostile.
edges.evidence_quote: The Lo Russo clan , then an important ally of the Alliance, disagreed and repackaged the shipment for sale on the streets, resulting in the deaths of many. ... The Lo Russo clan eventually split from the alliance, leading to a violent gang war against the other clans which claimed 120 lives in Naples and the surrounding region.

Los Caparrapos.description: Colombian narco-paramilitary group that was part of the internal armed conflict. Formed in 1996 as a personal guard for alias Macaco, later became part of the AUC and then the Clan del Golfo. Engaged in massacres, murders, extortion, forced displacement, drug trafficking, and illegal mining. The group was dismantled in 2021 with the death of its leader Robinson Gil Tapias.

descriptions: Clan del Golfo is at war with its dissident group Los Caparrapos.
edges.evidence_quote: una guerra entre el Clan del Golfo y su grupo disidente "Los Caparrapos"

descriptions: Clan del Golfo had a territorial dispute with Los Caparrapos.
edges.evidence_quote: Venía sosteniendo una disputa territorial con otras organizaciones armadas como la guerrilla del ELN y Los Caparrapos

descriptions: The Bloque Héroes de Castaño (a front of Clan del Golfo) is at war with Los Caparrapos, a dissident group of Clan del Golfo.
edges.evidence_quote: Se presume que serían ellos quienes están en guerra contra Los Caparrapos (grupo disidente del Clan del Golfo)

descriptions: Los Caparrapos were part of the AUC.
edges.evidence_quote: Este grupo y otros más harían parte de las Autodefensas Unidas de Colombia (AUC) de Vicente Castaño.

descriptions: Los Caparrapos broke their alliance with Clan del Golfo, leading to war from 2017.
edges.evidence_quote: lo que hizo romper su alianza con el Clan del Golfo desatando una guerra desde 2017

descriptions: Los Caparrapos are dissidents of the Clan del Golfo.
edges.evidence_quote: Son disidentes de las Autodefensas Gaitanistas de Colombia y/o Clan del Golfo.

descriptions: Los Caparrapos are possibly allied with FARC dissidents against Clan del Golfo.
edges.evidence_quote: Están posiblemente aliados con el ELN y las disidencias de las FARC-EP en su guerra contra el Clan del Golfo

descriptions: Los Caparrapos have alliances with CJNG.
edges.evidence_quote: Tienen alianzas con el Cartel Jalisco Nueva Generación de México.

descriptions: Los Caparrapos have pacts with Los Pachenca to target Clan del Golfo members.
edges.evidence_quote: ya tiene pactos con "Los Pachenca" para dar de baja a cualquier integrante del Clan del Golfo

descriptions: Survivors of Los Paisas joined Los Caparrapos.
edges.evidence_quote: Los Paisas fueron abatidos y detenidos por la Fuerza Pública, y los sobrevivientes se unieron a Los Caparrapos.

descriptions: Los Caparrapos are possibly allied with ELN against Clan del Golfo.
edges.evidence_quote: Están posiblemente aliados con el ELN y las disidencias de las FARC-EP en su guerra contra el Clan del Golfo

descriptions: Los Caparrapos sold their franchise to the Oficina de Envigado.
edges.evidence_quote: Los Caparrapos habrían vendido presuntamente su franquicia en el Bajo Cauca antioqueño a la Oficina de Envigado.

descriptions: Oficina de Envigado attempted to acquire Los Caparrapos to avoid disappearing.
edges.evidence_quote: viéndosen obligados, al parecer, a comprar a Los Caparrapos en el Bajo Cauca para no desaparecer

descriptions: Montreal Rockers was a support club for Outlaws MC and later became their chapter.
edges.evidence_quote: This particular group emerged as a supporter club for the Montreal chapter of the Outlaws MC before eventually working its way up to become their second chapter within the city in 1978.

VVT.description: Canadian Tamil street gang originating in Toronto, founded in the early 1990s by Sri Lankan Tamil immigrants. Named after the town of Valvettithurai in northern Sri Lanka. Involved in drug trafficking, fraud, weapons trafficking, counterfeiting, and extortion. The gang is now considered largely inactive.

descriptions: VVT and AK Aannan were engaged in a violent conflict (war) during this period.
edges.evidence_quote: In the mid 1990s to early 2000s, the VVT had an ongoing war with another Tamil gang called the AK Aannan based out of Scarborough.

descriptions: A temporary truce was established between VVT and AK Aannan in 1998.
edges.evidence_quote: There was a brief truce in 1998 after community members brokered a peace deal in a Richmond Hill Hindu temple; however, a murder a year later renewed the fighting.

descriptions: The Rugolo are a family group under the Mammoliti 'ndrina.
edges.evidence_quote: I Mammoliti sono una 'ndrina potente di Oppido Mamertina e Castellace quali fanno capo i Rugolo , i Nava, i Cosoleto, i Luppino e i Romeo con loro imparentate.
